svg.title: Qwen3 trace-derived runtime call-stack comparison
svg.desc: Measured prefill and median decode host ranges for auto-infer, omni-npu, and vllm-ascend.
svg.text: Decode: auto-infer 1.16× faster than omni-npu, 2.81× faster than vllm-ascend
Prefill is shown separately: vllm-ascend leads this captured window; stack depth alone is not treated as causality.
decode median 7.92 ms · max nesting 5 · baseline
decode median 9.20 ms · max nesting 7 · 1.16× slower
decode median 22.28 ms · max nesting 7 · 2.81× slower
Common x-scale within each row (ms). Bar position and width come from qwen3/call ranges in the raw trace; dN is measured nesting depth.
Observed target scope: auto-infer does not traverse the instrumented vLLM client/core or worker-wrapper/worker boundaries; decode is shorter.
What it does not prove: fewer Python boundaries alone caused the speedup. Device graph work is asynchronous and requires operator-level attribution.
Source: docs/profiling/qwen3/raw/*.trace.json · B16 · BF16 · 1 prefill + 15 continuous decode steps · profiler-instrumented host time
